metadata.title: Operation Spalax Water and Wastewater System Attack Scenario

Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Simple Basic attackers may use compromised websites or malicious ads to infect victims' devices with malware, exploiting weak passwords or lack of multi-factor authentication to gain access to water and wastewater systems, and then disrupt operations by deleting or corrupting critical system files or causing denial of service attacks.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, OS Process, Script File, Target Artifact (Tasks), Network Traffic, File
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Command and Scripting Interpreter, Scheduled Task/Job, Network Traffic Content (Mechanism), File Modification (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2022-32893 (Suggested): Apple iOS and macOS contain an out-of-bounds write vulnerability that could allow for remote code execution when processing malicious crafted web content.
stage.description: The attacker uses compromised websites or malicious ads to infect victims' devices with malware via drive-by download.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2018-8174 (Suggested): A remote code execution vulnerability exists in the way that the VBScript engine handles objects in memory, aka "Windows VBScript Engine Remote Code Execution"
stage.description: The attacker exploits weak passwords or lack of multi-factor authentication to gain access to water and wastewater systems using network logon scripts.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2013-5065 (Suggested): Microsoft Windows NDProxy.sys in the kernel contains an improper input validation vulnerability which can allow a local attacker to escalate privileges.
stage.description: The attacker executes commands on compromised systems using command-line interfaces or scripting engines.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2022-1040 (Suggested): An authentication bypass vulnerability in User Portal and Webadmin of Sophos Firewall allows for remote code execution.
stage.description: The attacker creates scheduled tasks to run malicious code at specific times, potentially disrupting water treatment processes.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker causes denial of service attacks by overloading systems with traffic or flooding them with requests.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attacker deletes or corrupts critical system files, leading to loss of operational control and potential harm to public health.

Scenario.summary: Operation Spalax attackers aim to disrupt water and wastewater systems by compromising initial access, executing malicious code, and causing impact through denial of service and data destruction.
